title: GPU not recognized with oci-nvidia-hook
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

On Fedora 30, I added the centos 7 repos from [here](https://nvidia.github.io/nvidia-container-runtime/) and installed nvidia-container-runtime-hook (which installs libnvidia-container and the oci hook).

oci-nvidia-hook.json is present in /etc/containers/oci/hooks.d, /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d, and /usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d along with the oci-nvidia-hook shell script in the last folder only.

When I try to pull any of the nvidia docker containers, my GPU is not visible to the container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install nvidia drivers (418), Cuda (10.0), and  nvidia-container-runtime-hook on Fedora 30

2. podman run --rm nvidia/cuda:9.0-base nvidia-smi

3. No GPU is found, hook is not loaded (see output of podman with debug log level [here](https://pastebin.com/PkBCXVCx)

**Describe the results you received:**

GPU not visible to the container.

**Describe the results you expected:**

GPU visible to the container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.2.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
From debugging this earlier, it sounded like the hook in question had its activation criteria set to "Always", but it doesn't appear to be registered by the hooks code.

@wking Mind taking a look at this one?
Can you attach the full hook JSON?

And probably also the Podman config, in case it set [custom hook paths][1].

[1]: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blame/v1.2.0/docs/libpod.conf.5.md#L33

The full hook is: 
```
  {
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "hook": {
          "path": "/usr/bin/nvidia-container-runtime-hook",
          "args": ["nvidia-container-runtime-hook", "prestart"],
          "env": [
              "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin              "
          ]
      },
      "when": {
          "always": true,
          "commands": [".*"]
      },
      "stages": ["prestart"]
  }
```

And my lipbod.conf

```
  volume_path = "/home/michael/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes"
  image_default_transport = "docker://"
  runtime = "runc"
  conmon_path = ["/usr/libexec/podman/conmon", "/usr/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon", "/usr/local/libexec/crio/conmon", "/usr/bin/conmon", "/usr/sbin/conmon", "/  usr/lib/podman/bin/conmon", "/usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon"]
  conmon_env_vars = ["PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"]
  cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
  init_path = "/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit"
  static_dir = "/home/michael/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod"
  tmp_dir = "/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp"
  max_log_size = -1
  no_pivot_root = false
  cni_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
  cni_plugin_dir = ["/usr/libexec/cni", "/usr/lib/cni", "/usr/local/lib/cni", "/opt/cni/bin"]
  infra_image = "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1"
  infra_command = "/pause"
  enable_port_reservation = true
  label = true
  network_cmd_path = ""
  num_locks = 2048
  EventsLogFilePath = ""

  [runtimes]
    runc = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc"]
```

It appears to be working when running podman as root with the --privileged flag.

Just in case anyone else runs into this problem on Fedora 30.

```
1. sudo dnf install https://download1.rpmfusion.org/free/fedora/rpmfusion-free-release-$(rpm -E %fedora).noarch.rpm https://download1.rpmfusion.org/nonfree/fedora/rpmfusion-nonfree-release-$(rpm -E %fedora).noarch.rpm

2. curl -s -L https://nvidia.github.io/nvidia-docker/centos7/nvidia-docker.repo | \
  sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/nvidia-docker.repo

3. sudo dnf install xorg-x11-drv-nvidia-cuda nvidia-container-runtime

4. sudo podman run --privileged --rm nvidia/cuda:9.0-base nvidia-smi

```

> It appears to be working when running podman as root with the --privileged flag.

So the issue is logging hook-execution to make it easier to see that lack-of-privilege is the problem?

Poking, no `hooks_dir` in the `libpod.conf`, which means we get [the defaults][1] (also, shame on your packager for relying on those defaults :wink:.  As the linked docs say, they're deprecated).  That means we're matching the highest-precedence `/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d` hook.  Dropping a dummy in for testing with Podman 0.12 and poking around I turned up #3233, although that's probably unrelated to this particular report.

[1]: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blame/v1.2.0/docs/podman.1.md#L46

Quote from:
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/podman.1.md

---

If `--hooks-dir` is unset for root callers, Podman and libpod will currently default to `/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d` and `/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d` in order of increasing precedence.  Using these defaults is deprecated, and callers should migrate to explicitly setting `--hooks-dir`.

---

As I interpret the documentation, it looks that your command 

`podman run --rm nvidia/cuda:9.0-base nvidia-smi`

will not run any hooks if it is run by a non-root user (i.e. run rootless).

And there is no `hooks_dir` in your `libpod.conf` that could change that.

I guess you need to either explicitly specify  `--hooks-dir=` as an option to the `podman run` command
or add `hooks_dir` to your `libpod.conf`.

We are adding `hooks_dir = ["/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d", "/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d"]
` to rootless libpod.conf automatically as of now - though that may be dependent on the contents of root's libpod.conf, which we use for defaulting rootless.
Ok I am going to assume this is fixed in podman1.5, @mjlbach reopen if I am mistaken.

I don't believe it is, but at the same time my impression is that the hook
in question requires root privileges?

On Sun, Aug 4, 2019, 06:32 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Closed #3155 <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3155>.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3155?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCH7QAVG2WH754KXN4TQC2V2RA5CNFSM4HN2VEA2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFWZEXG43VMVCXMZLOORHG65DJMZUWGYLUNFXW5KTDN5WW2ZLOORPWSZGOS3TLLBA#event-2531702148>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGM7FIXO7WUEIYUY7LQC2V2RANCNFSM4HN2VEAQ>
> .
>
Well a hook that requires root privs will not work with rootless mode.  One thing we might need to deal with in the future would be a way to disable some hooks when running in rootless mode.

> One thing we might need to deal with in the future would be a way to disable some hooks when running in rootless mode.

Configure a hook directory without those hooks in it?  Or add a higher-priority dir to the existing stack with masking, no-op hooks covering the root-only ones?

I am wondering if there is a way to do this in the homedir right now.  Basically allowing a rootless user to configure hooks that he wants to run, and eliminate hooks from the system that are not allowed to run in rootless mode.

I know this issue has already been closed, but the following tip might help anyone looking at this issue since it's pretty much the only documentation on the web for how to get `nvidia-container-runtime` working on Fedora 30+.

If you create `~/.config/containers/libpod.conf` with the contents below then you can run a GPU container as a normal user without having to add `--hooks-dir` each time you run `podman`.

```
hooks_dir = ["/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d", "/etc/containers/oci/hooks.d"]
```

Since it appears to extends the system `libpod.conf` this would be safer than modifying system files which may be replaced on package upgrades.  After making this change then the instructions provided in comment https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3155#issuecomment-497153536 by @mjlbach will work even without sudo in front.  It still requires `--privileged`, which wasn't true with docker (which instead required `--gpus=all`), but it's not too bad and best of all you can run a container without root access or adding yourself to a special group.

Probably because I'm running Fedora 31, which is using cgroups v2, I also had to edit `/etc/nvidia-container-runtime/config.toml` and set `no-cgroups = true`.
